blocks.description: Program header comments, LIBNAME definitions for src_a (ERP), src_b (DW), staging, and rpt libraries, %LET control parameters (recon_date, max_disc), and OPTIONS for macro debugging

Parameterized %MACRO reconcile_table(src_a=, src_b=, keys=, table_name=) — counts records in each source via PROC SQL, identifies missing records via LEFT JOINs, combines discrepancies into staging.recon_&table_name with source_system classification, and logs discrepancy counts
Invocation of %reconcile_table for customer master data: src_a.customers vs src_b.dim_customer keyed on customer_id
Invocation of %reconcile_table for order transactions: src_a.orders vs src_b.fact_orders keyed on order_id
Invocation of %reconcile_table for product catalog: src_a.products vs src_b.dim_product keyed on product_id
Open-code %IF/%ELSE conditional block: if &syserr > 0, logs error to staging.recon_error_log with process name and error message; otherwise emits success note
DATA step merging staging.recon_customers, staging.recon_orders, and staging.recon_products into rpt.consolidated_recon with severity classification (HIGH for MISSING_FROM_A, MEDIUM otherwise)
PROC SQL generating cross-table discrepancy count summary grouped by table_name and disc_type from rpt.consolidated_recon
PROC FREQ producing severity-by-table cross-tabulation from rpt.consolidated_recon
PROC PRINT listing top &max_disc discrepancy detail records from rpt.consolidated_recon
Title reset, LIBNAME CLEAR for src_a and src_b, and completion log message
